sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented for this shoot 'em up title

special_mechanics.name: Ship Control (Scrolling Shooter)
special_mechanics.description: Player controls D117B space fighter in alternating vertical-scrolling and horizontal-scrolling stages. No traditional ground-based movement physics apply; this is a projectile-based shooter.

special_mechanics.name: Weapon System
special_mechanics.description: Three selectable weapon types per stage: standard weapon, special weapon, and bomb/missile. Each weapon acts as a shield. Hit disables current weapon and reduces to weak default version; second hit while weakened destroys ship. Direct enemy collision still causes instant destruction.

special_mechanics.name: Respawn Mechanic
special_mechanics.description: Ship respawns immediately at death location rather than stage start. Limited lives and continues system.

special_mechanics.name: Visual Effects
special_mechanics.description: Pseudo-3D warping effect in top-down stages; parallax scrolling effects throughout.

tier_notes: Tier 2: Axelay is a shoot 'em up with no documented traditional platformer physics (jump, gravity, acceleration). Movement parameters are not applicable to this genre. Available sources (Wikipedia, TCRF) provide gameplay and development information but no quantitative physics data.
